methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $113M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Cresilon, a Series B biotech & health company founded by Joe Landolina, has raised $113M to date. Valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies operating in the same sector.

Two methods yielded applicable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $113M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $215M. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-stage, same-sector comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples could not be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, which is typical for private companies; the reference public median sits at 7.8x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $182M to $931M, with a median of $512M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($215M) and peer-median ($810M) reflects material uncertainty around whether Cresilon's profile aligns with high-performing outliers like Eikon or with the lower-quartile peers. Key uncertainties include undisclosed revenue trajectory, competitive positioning within the peer set, and burn rate relative to capital deployed. Further due diligence on revenue run-rate and product development stage would narrow this range materially.
